title: General Code Style Principles
summary: This document outlines general coding principles that apply across all languages and frameworks used in this project.

sections.heading: Readability
sections.body:
- Code should be easy to read and understand by humans.
- Avoid overly clever or obscure constructs.

sections.heading: Consistency
sections.body:
- Follow existing patterns in the codebase.
- Maintain consistent formatting, naming, and structure.

sections.heading: Simplicity
sections.body:
- Prefer simple solutions over complex ones.
- Break down complex problems into smaller, manageable parts.

sections.heading: Maintainability
sections.body:
- Write code that is easy to modify and extend.
- Minimize dependencies and coupling.

sections.heading: Documentation
sections.body:
- Document *why* something is done, not just *what*.
- Keep documentation up-to-date with code changes.

rules.summary: Code should be easy to read and understand by humans.
Avoid overly clever or obscure constructs.
Follow existing patterns in the codebase.
Maintain consistent formatting, naming, and structure.
Prefer simple solutions over complex ones.
Break down complex problems into smaller, manageable parts.
Write code that is easy to modify and extend.
Document *why* something is done, not just *what*.
Keep documentation up-to-date with code changes.
